message.content:
You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_48.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 48: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[48]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_48.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 48: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""I-deepfix-001 (KEYSTONE de-storm) — round-robin BURST-SPREAD start index for the per-claim
2	mirror-chain judge callers (``entailment_judge`` + ``credibility_judge_caller``).
3	
4	ROOT CAUSE this cures: both judges pin ONE mirror-chain host (``order=[chain[0]]``,
5	``allow_fallbacks=False``). Under the credibility pass's 20-way concurrency the old cursor-0 start
6	POSTed EVERY in-flight member to the SAME chain-lead host simultaneously -> an account-QPS 429 storm
7	on ONE host -> the pass blows its wall -> ``credibility_analysis=None`` (empty conclusion + W5
8	tiering starvation). This helper spreads the START host round-robin across the chain so
9	~``inflight / len(chain)`` members land on each host; a faulted call still walks the FULL ring
10	(wraparound) from wherever it started, so it can reach every healthy host before failing closed.
11	
12	TRANSPORT-ONLY, faithfulness-NEUTRAL. This picks WHICH healthy host answers; it NEVER changes the
13	glm-5.2 model, the prompt, temperature 0.0, max_tokens/reasoning effort, the JSON verdict parsing, or
14	the fail-closed sentinel. A timed-out / errored member still fails CLOSED (UNSUPPORTED) — it is
15	NEVER fake-stamped SUPPORTS. Same glm-5.2 model on every chain host (operator pre-approved judge-host
16	non-sovereignty 2026-06-13), so the verdict the answering host returns is the real verdict.
17	
18	LAW VI — env-gated, DEFAULT-ON, byte-identical OFF:
19	  * ``PG_JUDGE_BURST_SPREAD`` unset / ``1`` / ``true`` / ``on`` / ``spread`` -> "spread" (DEFAULT).
20	  * ``PG_JUDGE_BURST_SPREAD=0`` / ``false`` / ``no`` / ``off`` -> "off": cursor 0 + no-wrap ring stop
21	    == the pre-fix single-lead pin (byte-identical to the I-arch-011 rotation semantics).
22	  * ``PG_JUDGE_BURST_SPREAD=lb`` -> "lb": the documented D8-Judge cure — drop the single-host
23	    ``order`` and let OpenRouter LOAD-BALANCE the burst across all glm-5.2 endpoints. Opt-in fallback
24	    if round-robin start proves insufficient on the real snapshot; NOT the default.
25	"""
26	from __future__ import annotations
27	
28	import itertools
29	import os
30	import threading
31	
32	ENV_JUDGE_BURST_SPREAD = "PG_JUDGE_BURST_SPREAD"
33	
34	# Process-global round-robin counter shared by BOTH judge callers so the two bursts spread over the
35	# SAME chain in a coordinated way. Guarded by a lock so concurrent verify-workers draw DISTINCT
36	# consecutive integers (no lost increment -> a balanced spread). RISK-4 (benign): a multi-query
37	# process shares this counter — it still spreads; under one-query-per-VM it is moot.
38	_BURST_START = itertools.count()
39	_BURST_LOCK = threading.Lock()
40	
41	
42	def burst_spread_mode() -> str:
43	    """Resolve the burst-spread mode from ``PG_JUDGE_BURST_SPREAD``.
44	
45	    Returns one of ``"spread"`` (DEFAULT — round-robin start index), ``"off"`` (byte-identical
46	    single-lead pin), or ``"lb"`` (load-balance alternative). An unrecognized value defaults to
47	    ``"spread"`` (the de-storm stays ACTIVE — the safe direction)."""
48	    raw = os.environ.get(ENV_JUDGE_BURST_SPREAD, "").strip().lower()
49	    if raw in ("0", "false", "no", "off"):
50	        return "off"
51	    if raw == "lb":
52	        return "lb"
53	    return "spread"
54	
55	
56	def next_burst_start_index(chain_len: int) -> int:
57	    """Return the next round-robin START index in ``[0, chain_len)``.
58	
59	    Thread-safe: a lock guards the shared counter so concurrent verify-workers get DISTINCT
60	    consecutive integers, giving a balanced spread across the chain hosts. ``chain_len <= 1`` returns
61	    ``0`` (a single/empty chain has nothing to spread across)."""
62	    if chain_len <= 1:
63	        return 0
64	    with _BURST_LOCK:
65	        return next(_BURST_START) % chain_len
66	
67	
68	def _reset_burst_start(start: int = 0) -> None:
69	    """TEST-ONLY: reset the process-global round-robin counter to a known state so a test can assert a
70	    deterministic START host. Not called on any production path."""
71	    global _BURST_START  # noqa: PLW0603 — deliberate test hook for the shared counter
72	    with _BURST_LOCK:
73	        _BURST_START = itertools.count(start)
74

1	"""I-deepfix-001 (de-storm) — bounded, env-driven CONCURRENCY cap for the per-claim mirror-chain
2	side judges (``entailment_judge`` + ``credibility_judge_caller`` + ``semantic_conflict_detector``).
3	
4	ROOT CAUSE this cures (forensically observed on the 2026-07-04 super-serious deepener preflight,
5	``/root/deepener_preflight.log``): each of the three side judges fires a BLOCKING glm-5.2 mirror-chain
6	POST per claim, driven from a BOUNDED-PARALLEL verify / tiering / consolidation pool. But NOTHING
7	capped how many of those POSTs were in flight AT ONCE. Under the verify burst ~32 entailment POSTs
8	hit the mirror-chain LEAD host (``friendli``) at essentially the same instant — an account-QPS 429
9	storm on ONE host ("429 Too Many Requests" x32). The chain alternates (``novita`` / ``z-ai``) were
10	DNS-degraded on the box at the time ("Temporary failure in name resolution"), so provider rotation
11	kept landing back on ``friendli``; the per-claim judge then burned its retry budget on ~15-50s
12	rate-limit backoffs and, on exhaustion, emitted the fail-CLOSED ``('ENTAILED','judge_error: ...')``
13	sentinel. Consumers DROP that sentinel, so the entailment-requiring layers (the abstract / the
14	full-verify pass) collapsed to empty while the per-section body — verified earlier in smaller,
15	time-spread bursts — kept its claims.
16	
17	The CURE (transport-only, faithfulness-NEUTRAL): cap the number of side-judge provider POSTs that are
18	IN FLIGHT at once with a process-global bounded semaphore, SHARED across all three judges so the cap
19	is on TOTAL glm-5.2 mirror QPS (they all hit the SAME ``[friendli, novita, z-ai, phala]`` chain).
20	Fewer simultaneous POSTs per host -> fewer 429s -> fewer retry-exhaustion fail-closed drops -> MORE
21	claims verified with the SAME verdicts (a §-1.3 STRENGTHENING — it never relaxes faithfulness). This
22	composes with the existing ``judge_burst_spread`` round-robin START-host spread: with ``N`` in flight
23	spread over 4 hosts each host sees ~``N/4`` normally, and ``N`` is the worst-case ceiling if the
24	alternates are unreachable and every call rotates back to the lead.
25	
26	It changes ONLY *when* a POST is admitted — never the model, prompt, temperature, ``max_tokens``,
27	reasoning effort, JSON verdict parsing, provider rotation, or the fail-closed sentinel. A timed-out /
28	errored member still fails CLOSED exactly as before; this helper can neither manufacture nor flip a
29	verdict.
30	
31	LAW VI — env-driven (single knob, ``PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY``):
32	  * unset                       -> ``DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY`` (the de-storm is ON by default).
33	  * ``N`` (>= 1)                -> at most ``N`` side-judge POSTs in flight at once.
34	  * ``0``                       -> UNBOUNDED escape hatch: ``acquire_judge_slot()`` is a no-op
35	                                   context manager, byte-identical to the pre-fix behavior.
36	  * negative / malformed        -> ``DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY`` (a typo must not silently disable the
37	                                   storm protection — the safe direction).
38	
39	The bound is resolved at each ``acquire`` (so a test / bakeoff can re-point the env within one
40	process) and the process-global semaphore is (re)built lazily whenever the resolved bound changes,
41	guarded by a lock. Stdlib-only leaf module -> zero off-mode import cost.
42	"""
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import contextlib
46	import os
47	import threading
48	
49	ENV_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY = "PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY"
50	
51	# 4 mirror-chain hosts ([friendli, novita, z-ai, phala]); with burst-spread this is ~1 POST/host
52	# normally and a worst-case ceiling of 4 on the lead if the alternates are unreachable — comfortably
53	# below the ~32-simultaneous burst that 429-stormed friendli. Operators can raise this via the env if
54	# the provider capacity allows (the memory standard is "<= 4 agents in flight").
55	DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY = 4
56	
57	_LOCK = threading.Lock()
58	_SEMAPHORE: threading.BoundedSemaphore | None = None
59	_SEMAPHORE_BOUND: int | None = None
60	
61	# I-deepfix-001 (box2 credibility-pass SPEED fix): a PROCESS-GLOBAL, thread-safe PHASE OVERRIDE. When
62	# set (> 0) it REPLACES the env-resolved cap for the DURATION of a wrapped phase — used to raise the
63	# side-judge concurrency ONLY while the advisory credibility pass runs (both its legs: the credibility
64	# scorer AND the basket-member entailment verify go through ``acquire_judge_slot``), leaving the
65	# composition-time entailment cap at its protected env value. Default None => no override => the env
66	# path below is byte-identical. Set/cleared by ``credibility_pass_concurrency`` around the pass; on the
67	# one-query-per-VM model no two passes overlap in a process.
68	_OVERRIDE_LOCK = threading.Lock()
69	_CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE: int | None = None
70	
71	
72	class JudgeSlotTimeout(RuntimeError):
73	    """Raised by ``acquire_judge_slot(timeout=…)`` when a slot is not admitted within the deadline.
74	
75	    A wedged slot-holder (e.g. the documented GIL/fut.result stall) can no longer freeze the run: an
76	    ADVISORY caller (the credibility judge) catches this and degrades THAT row to a disclosed
77	    ``judge_error`` priors fallback. Binding callers that pass no timeout are unaffected (default
78	    ``None`` == the pre-fix unbounded acquire)."""
79	
80	
81	def resolve_max_concurrency() -> int:
82	    """Resolve the configured in-flight side-judge cap.
83	
84	    Precedence: an active ``credibility_pass_concurrency`` PHASE OVERRIDE (> 0) wins; otherwise the
85	    env ``PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY``. Returns the cap ``N >= 1`` when bounded, or ``0`` for
86	    UNBOUNDED (the explicit ``"0"`` escape hatch). Unset -> ``DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY``. A negative or
87	    malformed value -> ``DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY`` (a typo must not silently disable the de-storm).
88	    Never raises (a transport-config read must not break the judge)."""
89	    with _OVERRIDE_LOCK:
90	        override = _CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE
91	    if override is not None and override > 0:
92	        return override
93	    raw = os.environ.get(ENV_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY, "").strip()
94	    if raw == "":
95	        return DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY
96	    try:
97	        n = int(raw)
98	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
99	        return DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY
100	    if n == 0:
101	        return 0  # explicit unbounded escape hatch (byte-identical to the pre-fix behavior)
102	    if n < 0:
103	        return DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY
104	    return n
105	
106	
107	@contextlib.contextmanager
108	def credibility_pass_concurrency(bound: int | None):
109	    """Temporarily REPLACE the side-judge concurrency cap for the wrapped block (the credibility pass).
110	
111	    ``bound`` <= 0 / None => no override (byte-identical: the env cap governs). ``bound`` >= 1 raises
112	    the effective cap to exactly ``bound`` for the DURATION of the block — every ``acquire_judge_slot``
113	    call on any thread reads it via ``resolve_max_concurrency``. Restores the prior value on EVERY exit
114	    (success or exception). Faithfulness-neutral: it changes only HOW MANY advisory/entailment POSTs are
115	    admitted at once during this ONE phase; it never touches the model, prompt, verdict, or gate. Leaves
116	    the composition-time side-judge cap (the env value) untouched, so the storm protection there holds.
117	    The (re)built semaphore is swapped atomically per ``_get_semaphore``; in-flight holders release the
118	    object they acquired, so a mid-flight bound change never corrupts a live acquire."""
119	    global _CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE
120	    try:
121	        n = int(bound) if bound is not None else 0
122	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
123	        n = 0
124	    if n <= 0:
125	        yield
126	        return
127	    with _OVERRIDE_LOCK:
128	        prev = _CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE
129	        _CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE = n
130	    try:
131	        yield
132	    finally:
133	        with _OVERRIDE_LOCK:
134	            _CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE = prev
135	
136	
137	def _get_semaphore(bound: int) -> threading.BoundedSemaphore:
138	    """Return the process-global ``BoundedSemaphore`` for ``bound`` (>= 1), rebuilding it iff the
139	    resolved bound changed since the last call. In production the bound is constant so the semaphore
140	    is built exactly once; a test that re-points the env gets a fresh semaphore. In-flight holders
141	    release the object they acquired, so a rebuild never corrupts a live acquire."""
142	    global _SEMAPHORE, _SEMAPHORE_BOUND
143	    with _LOCK:
144	        if _SEMAPHORE is None or _SEMAPHORE_BOUND != bound:
145	            _SEMAPHORE = threading.BoundedSemaphore(bound)
146	            _SEMAPHORE_BOUND = bound
147	        return _SEMAPHORE
148	
149	
150	@contextlib.contextmanager
151	def acquire_judge_slot(timeout: float | None = None):
152	    """Admit at most ``resolve_max_concurrency()`` side-judge provider POSTs concurrently.
153	
154	    Wrap the blocking provider POST in ``with acquire_judge_slot():``. When the cap is ``0``
155	    (unbounded escape hatch) this is a no-op — byte-identical to the pre-fix path. Otherwise it
156	    acquires one slot of the shared process-global bounded semaphore for the duration of the POST and
157	    releases it on EVERY exit path (success, timeout, or exception), so a hung/timed-out call frees
158	    its slot when its total-deadline force-closes it. TRANSPORT-ONLY: it never touches the verdict.
159	
160	    ``timeout`` (I-deepfix-001 box2 fix): when a positive deadline is supplied and no slot is admitted
161	    within it, raise ``JudgeSlotTimeout`` BEFORE the POST (nothing acquired => nothing released). This
162	    is the graceful-degrade path for the ADVISORY credibility judge: a wedged slot-holder can no longer
163	    strangle the run — the timed-out row falls back to a disclosed ``judge_error`` prior. ``None`` (the
164	    default, used by every binding caller) keeps the pre-fix UNBOUNDED acquire, byte-identical."""
165	    bound = resolve_max_concurrency()
166	    if bound <= 0:
167	        yield
168	        return
169	    sem = _get_semaphore(bound)
170	    if timeout is not None and timeout > 0:
171	        if not sem.acquire(timeout=timeout):
172	            raise JudgeSlotTimeout(
173	                f"side-judge slot not admitted within {timeout:g}s (cap={bound}) — degrading this "
174	                "advisory judge call to a disclosed judge_error prior so a wedged slot-holder can "
175	                "never freeze the run (transport-only; no verdict/gate touched)"
176	            )
177	    else:
178	        sem.acquire()
179	    try:
180	        yield
181	    finally:
182	        sem.release()
183	
184	
185	def _reset_for_test() -> None:
186	    """TEST-ONLY: drop the process-global semaphore so the next ``acquire`` rebuilds it from the
187	    current env. Not called on any production path."""
188	    global _SEMAPHORE, _SEMAPHORE_BOUND
189	    with _LOCK:
190	        _SEMAPHORE = None
191	        _SEMAPHORE_BOUND = None
192

1	"""I-deepfix-001 — shared reasoning-block builder for the reasoning-model side judges.
2	
3	§9.1.8 (I-arch-003 #1253, operator-locked 2026-06-13): a reasoning model emits its internal reasoning
4	burst FIRST, drawn from the SAME ``max_tokens`` budget as the answer. If a provider lets reasoning run up
5	to the full budget, the JSON verdict never lands and ``message.content`` comes back EMPTY (finish_reason=
6	``length``). The operator's live probe proved this on ``z-ai/glm-5.2`` (max_tokens=20 -> content=None; the
7	reasoning burst ate the whole budget; max_tokens=300 -> content='OK'). A starved verdict then either
8	fails-closed (quality degrade) or, on a slow pinned provider, stalls.
9	
10	THE PROVEN CURE is NOT "raise max_tokens" (already generous at 131072) — it is a NUMERIC
11	``reasoning.max_tokens`` cap STRICTLY BELOW the total ``max_tokens`` so the verdict ALWAYS keeps at least
12	``content_floor`` tokens no matter how long a provider runs reasoning. This is exactly what the D8 Mirror
13	does (``roles/openrouter_role_transport`` mirror branch, EMPIRICALLY validated 2026-06-14 by
14	``scripts/diagnostics/mirror_glm_provider_bakeoff.py``: GLM at reasoning_cap=100000 / max_tokens=131072
15	returned 3/3 clean, finish=stop, on ALL of atlas-cloud/z-ai/baidu/novita/gmicloud). The three glm-family
16	side judges (credibility / entailment / semantic-conflict) never got this cap — they relied on
17	``reasoning:{effort:...}``, and ``effort`` is a NO-OP on GLM (its OpenRouter endpoints do not list it), so
18	a provider that runs reasoning long could still blank them. This leaf helper carries the ONE proven
19	invariant to all three, keeping the fix in a single place.
20	
21	Model-aware (mirrors ``roles/openrouter_role_transport._judge_reasoning_block`` semantics):
22	  * glm family -> ``{"max_tokens": reasoning_cap}``  numeric cap; reasoning STAYS ON; content guaranteed.
23	  * otherwise  -> ``{"effort": effort}``             BYTE-IDENTICAL to the prior side-judge default (e.g.
24	                                                     the kimi-k2.6 entailment override keeps its proven
25	                                                     effort-tier shape; kimi's own 21-provider breadth +
26	                                                     small reasoning burst already prevent starvation).
27	
28	FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: reasoning stays ON for every reasoning model; only HOW glm reasoning is bounded
29	changes (a numeric ceiling instead of an ignored effort tier). No verdict logic, no model, no drop.
30	LAW VI: the reasoning cap and content floor are env-overridable named knobs (no magic numbers).
31	
32	Leaf module — stdlib only, no heavy imports — so the off-mode side judges pay zero import cost.
33	"""
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import logging
37	import os
38	
39	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.judge_reasoning_block")
40	
41	# LAW VI (env-overridable, no magic numbers). Defaults mirror the PROVEN D8 Mirror bound
42	# (reasoning_cap 100000, content floor 4000 => the invariant reasoning_cap <= max_tokens - 4000).
43	_ENV_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS = "PG_JUDGE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS"
44	_DEFAULT_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS = 100000
45	_ENV_REASONING_CONTENT_FLOOR = "PG_JUDGE_REASONING_CONTENT_FLOOR"
46	_DEFAULT_REASONING_CONTENT_FLOOR = 4000
47	# Absolute lower bound for the reasoning cap: a per-claim NLI/credibility burst is ~300 tokens live, so
48	# 1024 is already ~3x headroom while guaranteeing the cap can never collapse to a starving value even if
49	# a bad env pushes max_tokens very low.
50	_REASONING_CAP_FLOOR = 1024
51	
52	# glm-family slug detection. The lock pins generator+mirror (and the legacy_compat side judges) to
53	# ``z-ai/glm-5.2``; other glm hosts/mirrors may carry the family in the slug (``glm``) or a known vendor
54	# prefix. Substring + prefix keeps it robust to a provider-scoped slug.
55	_GLM_SLUG_PREFIXES = ("z-ai/", "zhipuai/", "zhipu/", "thudm/")
56	
57	
58	def _int_env(name: str, default: int) -> int:
59	    try:
60	        value = int(os.environ.get(name, "") or default)
61	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
62	        return default
63	    return value if value > 0 else default
64	
65	
66	def is_glm_slug(slug: str | None) -> bool:
67	    """True iff ``slug`` names a GLM-family model (the family that ignores ``effort`` and needs the
68	    numeric reasoning cap)."""
69	    if not slug:
70	        return False
71	    s = str(slug).strip().lower()
72	    return "glm" in s or s.startswith(_GLM_SLUG_PREFIXES)
73	
74	
75	_STARVED_BUDGET_WARNED = False
76	
77	
78	def _warn_starved_budget_once(total: int, floor: int) -> None:
79	    """Warn LOUDLY (once per process) that a reasoning judge was configured with a total
80	    ``max_tokens`` too small to keep the full content floor — a §9.1.8 misconfiguration
81	    ("max_tokens ALWAYS go to the model REAL max"). Warn-once avoids per-claim log spam."""
82	    global _STARVED_BUDGET_WARNED
83	    if _STARVED_BUDGET_WARNED:
84	        return
85	    _STARVED_BUDGET_WARNED = True
86	    logger.warning(
87	        "[judge_reasoning_block] STARVED reasoning budget: total max_tokens=%d is below the "
88	        "healthy floor (content_floor=%d + reasoning_cap_floor=%d = %d). Per CLAUDE.md §9.1.8 a "
89	        "reasoning judge's max_tokens must go to the model REAL max; this tiny budget degrades the "
90	        "reasoning burst. Bounding reasoning STRICTLY BELOW the total so content can NEVER blank — "
91	        "but RAISE the judge's max_tokens env knob.",
92	        total, floor, _REASONING_CAP_FLOOR, floor + _REASONING_CAP_FLOOR,
93	    )
94	
95	
96	def reasoning_cap_for(max_tokens: int) -> int:
97	    """The numeric reasoning ceiling for a glm judge given its total ``max_tokens`` budget.
98	
99	    HARD INVARIANT (Codex I-deepfix-001 diff-gate iter1 P1): the returned cap is ALWAYS STRICTLY
100	    BELOW ``max_tokens`` — reasoning can never consume the entire budget, so the verdict JSON always
101	    keeps at least some content headroom. Two regimes:
102	
103	      * HEALTHY budget (``max_tokens - content_floor >= _REASONING_CAP_FLOOR``): reserve the FULL
104	        ``content_floor`` for the verdict and cap reasoning at ``min(cap_env, max_tokens - floor)``
105	        (<= max_tokens - floor, hence strictly below max_tokens). The 131072 mirror-chain-min budget
106	        every side judge uses lands here (cap 100000, floor 4000 => 31072 tokens of content headroom).
107	      * STARVED budget (a §9.1.8 MISCONFIGURATION — a reasoning judge should never run this small):
108	        the full content floor no longer fits. We STILL never let reasoning eat the whole budget —
109	        reasoning is bounded to ~half (strictly below max_tokens; content keeps the other half) and a
110	        loud warn-once fires so the bad token knob is visible. Fixes the prior inversion where the
111	        1024 floor could exceed a low total (e.g. the 256-token relevance-judge starvation config),
112	        making ``reasoning.max_tokens >= max_tokens`` and re-opening the blank-verdict path.
113	
114	    Env-overridable per LAW VI (``PG_JUDGE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS`` / ``PG_JUDGE_REASONING_CONTENT_FLOOR``).
115	    """
116	    cap_env = _int_env(_ENV_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS, _DEFAULT_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS)
117	    floor = _int_env(_ENV_REASONING_CONTENT_FLOOR, _DEFAULT_REASONING_CONTENT_FLOOR)
118	    try:
119	        total = int(max_tokens)
120	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
121	        total = 0
122	    headroom = total - floor
123	    if headroom >= _REASONING_CAP_FLOOR:
124	        # cap <= headroom = total - floor < total  -> strictly below AND full content floor kept.
125	        return min(cap_env, headroom)
126	    # Starved budget: the content floor no longer fits. Keep reasoning STRICTLY below the total so
127	    # SOME content room always remains, and warn loudly (this violates §9.1.8's max-budget lock).
128	    _warn_starved_budget_once(total, floor)
129	    if total <= 1:
130	        # Degenerate (never a real config: every side judge clamps its total >= 64). Nothing sensible
131	        # fits; return the smallest positive cap. Real callers never send total <= 1.
132	        return 1
133	    return max(1, min(total - 1, total // 2))
134	
135	
136	def build_judge_reasoning_block(model_slug: str | None, effort: object, max_tokens: int) -> dict:
137	    """Build the ``reasoning`` block for a reasoning-model side judge's OpenRouter body.
138	
139	    * A GLM-family judge gets a NUMERIC ``{"max_tokens": reasoning_cap}`` ceiling (the proven Mirror
140	      pattern) so the verdict can never be blanked by reasoning eating the budget — the §9.1.8 fix.
141	    * Any other model keeps the prior ``{"effort": effort}`` shape (byte-identical), so the kimi-k2.6
142	      entailment override and any future non-glm judge are UNCHANGED.
143	
144	    Faithfulness-neutral, transport-only. ``effort`` is passed through verbatim for the non-glm path.
145	    """
146	    if is_glm_slug(model_slug):
147	        return {"max_tokens": reasoning_cap_for(max_tokens)}
148	    return {"effort": effort}
149

1	"""I-deepfix-001 fix I3 — per-run verdict idempotency cache for the runtime faithfulness judge.
2	
3	WHY THIS EXISTS (the ~178-calls-per-report availability problem)
4	----------------------------------------------------------------
5	The entailment judge (``entailment_judge._EntailmentJudge.judge``) is the runtime faithfulness gate's
6	LLM leg (strict_verify check (f)). A drb_72-class report fires it ~178 times under ThreadPool concurrency,
7	and the SAME ``(claim sentence, cited span)`` pair recurs across sections (a consolidated basket's member
8	span is re-verified everywhere the claim is composed, and identical corroborator spans repeat). Every one
9	of those repeats is a fresh OpenRouter call that (a) costs money and (b) adds load to the judge provider
10	pool — the exact pressure that 429s the judge and OVER-DROPS grounded claims (the render-blocker I3
11	targets).
12	
13	THE FIX — a process-local, per-(model, normalized-claim, normalized-span, prompt-variant) verdict cache
14	------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
15	An identical ``(model, sentence, span, prompt_variant)`` tuple is a deterministic input to the judge
16	(temperature=0.0), so its verdict is idempotent. Caching the FIRST real verdict and serving every
17	identical later call from the cache removes the redundant network calls without changing any verdict. The
18	resolved-prompt variant is part of the key so a same-process prompt bakeoff (which switches the entailment
19	prompt per call) never serves one variant's verdict for another variant's prompt.
20	
21	FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL, by construction
22	-------------------------------------
23	  * The cache returns the SAME ``(verdict, reason)`` the judge already produced for that exact input —
24	    it can never manufacture, relax, or flip a verdict. The downstream strict_verify / provenance
25	    consumers run byte-identically on the cached tuple (same telemetry, same drop/keep decision).
26	  * A fail-CLOSED sentinel (``verdict == 'ENTAILED'`` AND ``reason`` starts ``judge_error:``) is NEVER
27	    cached — a transient judge fault must stay retryable, so a later identical call still gets a fresh
28	    attempt at a REAL verdict. ``put`` refuses the sentinel defensively even if a caller mis-calls it.
29	  * Only the two legit terminal verdicts on a real response are cached: NEUTRAL / CONTRADICTED, and
30	    ENTAILED whose reason is NOT a ``judge_error:`` sentinel.
31	
32	SCOPE / LIFETIME
33	----------------
34	Process-local (in-memory), NOT on disk: a cached verdict is only trusted within the process that produced
35	it, so there is zero staleness / cross-run-poisoning surface. ``reset_cache`` clears it (tests + a fresh
36	run boundary). Bounded by ``PG_JUDGE_VERDICT_CACHE_MAX`` with FIFO eviction so a long-lived process cannot
37	grow the map without bound.
38	
39	LAW VI: every knob is env-driven.
40	  * ``PG_JUDGE_VERDICT_CACHE``      default ``1`` (on). ``0``/``false``/``no``/``off`` => the cache is a
41	    no-op (``get`` always misses, ``put`` never stores) => the judge path is byte-identical to pre-I3.
42	  * ``PG_JUDGE_VERDICT_CACHE_MAX``  default ``100000`` entries; FIFO eviction of the oldest above the cap.
43	
44	Leaf module — stdlib only (``hashlib``/``os``/``threading``/``collections``) — so off-mode side judges pay
45	zero import cost.
46	"""
47	from __future__ import annotations
48	
49	import hashlib
50	import os
51	import re
52	import threading
53	from collections import OrderedDict
54	from typing import Optional, Tuple
55	
56	_ENV_ENABLED = "PG_JUDGE_VERDICT_CACHE"
57	_ENV_MAX = "PG_JUDGE_VERDICT_CACHE_MAX"
58	_DEFAULT_MAX = 100000
59	
60	_TRUE = {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
61	_FALSE = {"0", "false", "no", "off"}
62	
63	# collapse any run of unicode whitespace to a single ASCII space (normalization only merges inputs that
64	# are the SAME claim/span modulo whitespace — never two distinct claims).
65	_WS = re.compile(r"\s+")
66	
67	# module-level store (process-lifetime, shared across the ThreadPool verifier workers via the lock).
68	_LOCK = threading.RLock()
69	_STORE: "OrderedDict[str, Tuple[str, str]]" = OrderedDict()
70	
71	# observability counters (read by the behavioral test + any run telemetry). Not faithfulness state.
72	_STATS = {"hits": 0, "misses": 0, "stores": 0, "skipped_sentinel": 0, "evictions": 0}
73	
74	
75	def cache_enabled() -> bool:
76	    """True unless ``PG_JUDGE_VERDICT_CACHE`` is an explicit falsey value. Default ON."""
77	    raw = os.environ.get(_ENV_ENABLED, "").strip().lower()
78	    if raw in _FALSE:
79	        return False
80	    # unset or any truthy value => enabled (default-on).
81	    return True
82	
83	
84	def _max_entries() -> int:
85	    try:
86	        v = int(os.environ.get(_ENV_MAX, "").strip() or _DEFAULT_MAX)
87	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
88	        return _DEFAULT_MAX
89	    return v if v > 0 else _DEFAULT_MAX
90	
91	
92	def _normalize(text: object) -> str:
93	    return _WS.sub(" ", str(text)).strip()
94	
95	
96	def make_key(model: str, sentence: str, span: str, prompt_variant: str = "") -> str:
97	    """Stable content key for a ``(model, sentence, span, prompt_variant)`` tuple.
98	
99	    Whitespace-normalized and hashed (sha256) so the map holds fixed-size keys instead of full span text.
100	    The components are joined with a delimiter that cannot appear inside a single component after
101	    normalization collapses whitespace, so distinct tuples cannot collide by concatenation.
102	
103	    ``prompt_variant`` (I3 P1 fix, Fable gate iter1): the judge's verdict is a function of the RESOLVED
104	    entailment PROMPT, not just the (model, sentence, span). The prompt is a call-time variable
105	    (``PG_ENTAILMENT_PROMPT_VARIANT`` is read per call so the widening bakeoff can switch variants in ONE
106	    process). Omitting the variant from the key would make a same-process bakeoff serve variant-1's verdict
107	    for every later variant — a silent WRONG measurement. Including the active variant id makes each
108	    distinct prompt a distinct cache decision. Default ``""`` (single-variant production runs) keeps the
109	    key stable across the whole run."""
110	    norm = "\x1f".join(
111	        (_normalize(model), _normalize(sentence), _normalize(span), _normalize(prompt_variant))
112	    )
113	    return hashlib.sha256(norm.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
114	
115	
116	def _is_fail_closed_sentinel(verdict: str, reason: str) -> bool:
117	    """The entailment judge's fail-CLOSED terminal sentinel: ('ENTAILED', 'judge_error: <reason>').
118	    Never cached (a transient judge fault must stay retryable)."""
119	    return str(verdict).strip().upper() == "ENTAILED" and str(reason).lstrip().startswith("judge_error:")
120	
121	
122	def get(model: str, sentence: str, span: str, prompt_variant: str = "") -> Optional[Tuple[str, str]]:
123	    """Return the cached ``(verdict, reason)`` for this exact tuple, or ``None`` on a miss / when the
124	    cache is disabled. ``prompt_variant`` is part of the key (see ``make_key``) so a different resolved
125	    entailment prompt is a distinct cache decision. A hit refreshes recency (moves the entry to the FIFO
126	    tail is NOT done — FIFO is by INSERTION order, not access, so the eviction policy stays predictable)."""
127	    if not cache_enabled():
128	        return None
129	    key = make_key(model, sentence, span, prompt_variant)
130	    with _LOCK:
131	        val = _STORE.get(key)
132	        if val is None:
133	            _STATS["misses"] += 1
134	            return None
135	        _STATS["hits"] += 1
136	        # return a copy-safe tuple (tuples are immutable, so the stored object is safe to hand back).
137	        return val
138	
139	
140	def put(
141	    model: str,
142	    sentence: str,
143	    span: str,
144	    verdict: str,
145	    reason: str,
146	    prompt_variant: str = "",
147	) -> bool:
148	    """Store a REAL terminal verdict for this tuple. Returns True iff stored. ``prompt_variant`` is part
149	    of the key (see ``make_key``) so a verdict is only ever served back for the SAME resolved prompt.
150	
151	    Refuses (returns False) when the cache is disabled OR the value is the fail-closed
152	    ('ENTAILED','judge_error:…') sentinel (defensive: a transient fault must never be memoized)."""
153	    if not cache_enabled():
154	        return False
155	    if _is_fail_closed_sentinel(verdict, reason):
156	        with _LOCK:
157	            _STATS["skipped_sentinel"] += 1
158	        return False
159	    key = make_key(model, sentence, span, prompt_variant)
160	    with _LOCK:
161	        if key in _STORE:
162	            # already cached (a concurrent worker won the race) — keep the first, do not double-count.
163	            return True
164	        _STORE[key] = (str(verdict), str(reason))
165	        _STATS["stores"] += 1
166	        cap = _max_entries()
167	        while len(_STORE) > cap:
168	            _STORE.popitem(last=False)  # FIFO: evict the oldest insertion
169	            _STATS["evictions"] += 1
170	        return True
171	
172	
173	def reset_cache() -> None:
174	    """Clear the cache + zero the stats. Call at a fresh-run boundary and in tests."""
175	    with _LOCK:
176	        _STORE.clear()
177	        for k in _STATS:
178	            _STATS[k] = 0
179	
180	
181	def stats() -> dict:
182	    """Read-only snapshot of the observability counters + current size."""
183	    with _LOCK:
184	        snap = dict(_STATS)
185	        snap["size"] = len(_STORE)
186	        return snap
187

content.text: These four files are clean, professional naming despite verbose docstrings. Now let's read the larger files.

content.content:
1	"""Human-in-the-loop LLM client that replaces OpenRouterClient.
2	
3	The pipeline runs normally. At every LLM call site, instead of an HTTP request
4	to OpenRouter, the prompt is written to disk under loopback/pending/. The call
5	blocks until a corresponding response file appears under loopback/responses/.
6	The operator (or a separate Claude session) reads the prompt files, generates
7	responses by hand, and writes them to disk. The pipeline picks them up and
8	continues.
9	
10	This is the "Claude Code as the LLM" approach: zero OpenRouter cost; the entire
11	pipeline executes end-to-end with a real LLM in the loop, surfacing every
12	code-bug, schema mismatch, and state-machine regression at the cost of the
13	operator's time.
14	
15	Drop-in replacement: same constructor signature, same generate / generate_structured
16	async methods, same async-context-manager semantics, same .usage and .model
17	attributes that the rest of the codebase reads.
18	
19	Activated by setting `PG_LOOPBACK_MODE=1`. Detection happens in graph.py at
20	client construction time (no other code paths need to change).
21	"""
22	
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import asyncio
26	import json
27	import logging
28	import os
29	import time
30	import uuid
31	from pathlib import Path
32	from typing import Any, Optional, Type, TypeVar
33	
34	from pydantic import BaseModel
35	
36	from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
37	    LLMResponse,
38	    UsageTracker,
39	)
40	
41	T = TypeVar("T", bound=BaseModel)
42	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
43	
44	LOOPBACK_DIR = Path(os.getenv("PG_LOOPBACK_DIR", "loopback"))
45	PENDING_DIR = LOOPBACK_DIR / "pending"
46	RESPONSES_DIR = LOOPBACK_DIR / "responses"
47	DONE_DIR = LOOPBACK_DIR / "done"
48	POLL_INTERVAL_SEC = 0.5
49	DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SEC = float(os.getenv("PG_LOOPBACK_TIMEOUT_SEC", "7200"))  # 2h per call
50	
51	
52	class LoopbackLLMClient:
53	    """Drop-in OpenRouterClient replacement for human-in-the-loop testing."""
54	
55	    _billing_exhausted = False  # mirror class-level OpenRouterClient flag
56	
57	    def __init__(
58	        self,
59	        api_key: Optional[str] = None,
60	        model: Optional[str] = None,
61	        budget_usd: Optional[float] = None,
62	        session_id: str = "",
63	    ) -> None:
64	        self.model = model or "loopback/operator"
65	        self.usage = UsageTracker(
66	            budget_usd=budget_usd or 0.0,  # no real budget — operator is free
67	            session_id=session_id,
68	        )
69	        for d in (PENDING_DIR, RESPONSES_DIR, DONE_DIR):
70	            d.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
71	        logger.info(
72	            "[LOOPBACK] Initialized — pending=%s, responses=%s, done=%s",
73	            PENDING_DIR, RESPONSES_DIR, DONE_DIR,
74	        )
75	
76	    async def __aenter__(self):
77	        return self
78	
79	    async def __aexit__(self, *args):
80	        return None
81	
82	    async def close(self):
83	        return None
84	
85	    async def validate_reasoning(self) -> bool:
86	        """Loopback always reports reasoning OK; we don't validate here."""
87	        return True
88	
89	    async def generate(
90	        self,
91	        prompt: str,
92	        system: str = "",
93	        max_tokens: int = 4096,
94	        temperature: float = 0.7,
95	        timeout: Optional[float] = None,
96	        reasoning_max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
97	        reasoning_exclude: Optional[bool] = None,
98	    ) -> LLMResponse:
99	        """Block until operator writes a response file."""
100	        return await self._loopback_call(
101	            prompt=prompt,
102	            system=system,
103	            call_type="generate",
104	            schema_name=None,
105	            schema_json=None,
106	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
107	            temperature=temperature,
108	            timeout=timeout,
109	            reasoning_exclude=reasoning_exclude,
110	        )
111	
112	    async def reason(
113	        self,
114	        prompt: str,
115	        system: str = "",
116	        schema: Optional[Type[T]] = None,
117	        effort: str = "high",
118	        max_tokens: int = 16384,
119	        timeout: Optional[float] = None,
120	        reasoning_max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
121	        reasoning_exclude: Optional[bool] = None,
122	    ) -> LLMResponse:
123	        """Analysis/planning call — loopback version.
124	
125	        OpenRouterClient separates reasoning from content. For loopback the
126	        operator writes a single response; we put it in .content and leave
127	        .reasoning empty unless the operator explicitly supplied one. Callers
128	        that check response.reasoning get an empty string; callers that read
129	        response.content get the operator's answer. This matches the
130	        production behavior when reasoning_exclude=True.
131	        """
132	        call_type = "reason"
133	        if schema is not None:
134	            call_type = f"reason:{schema.__name__}"
135	            try:
136	                schema_json = schema.model_json_schema()
137	            except Exception:
138	                schema_json = {"name": schema.__name__}
139	            response = await self._loopback_call(
140	                prompt=prompt,
141	                system=system,
142	                call_type=call_type,
143	                schema_name=schema.__name__,
144	                schema_json=schema_json,
145	                max_tokens=max_tokens,
146	                temperature=0.0,
147	                timeout=timeout,
148	                reasoning_exclude=reasoning_exclude,
149	            )
150	            # The schema branch exists so reason(schema=X) callers can still
151	            # parse — but reason() historically returned LLMResponse, not a
152	            # schema instance. Preserve that contract; callers that want a
153	            # schema instance should use generate_structured().
154	            return response
155	        return await self._loopback_call(
156	            prompt=prompt,
157	            system=system,
158	            call_type=call_type,
159	            schema_name=None,
160	            schema_json=None,
161	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
162	            temperature=0.0,
163	            timeout=timeout,
164	            reasoning_exclude=reasoning_exclude,
165	        )
166	
167	    async def generate_structured(
168	        self,
169	        prompt: str,
170	        schema: Type[T],
171	        system: str = "",
172	        max_tokens: int = 8192,
173	        timeout: Optional[float] = None,
174	        reasoning_enabled: bool = False,
175	        reasoning_effort: str = "high",
176	        reasoning_max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
177	        reasoning_exclude: Optional[bool] = None,
178	    ) -> T:
179	        """Block until operator writes a response file. Parse as schema."""
180	        try:
181	            schema_json = schema.model_json_schema()
182	        except Exception:
183	            schema_json = {"name": schema.__name__}
184	
185	        response = await self._loopback_call(
186	            prompt=prompt,
187	            system=system,
188	            call_type=f"structured:{schema.__name__}",
189	            schema_name=schema.__name__,
190	            schema_json=schema_json,
191	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
192	            temperature=0.0,
193	            timeout=timeout,
194	            reasoning_exclude=reasoning_exclude,
195	        )
196	
197	        content = response.content.strip()
198	        # Strip optional code fences the operator might leave in
199	        if content.startswith("```"):
200	            lines = content.split("\n")
201	            content = "\n".join(lines[1:-1] if lines[-1].startswith("```") else lines[1:])
202	            content = content.strip()
203	
204	        try:
205	            return schema.model_validate_json(content)
206	        except Exception as exc:
207	            logger.error(
208	                "[LOOPBACK] Failed to parse response as %s: %s\nRaw: %s",
209	                schema.__name__, exc, content[:500],
210	            )
211	            raise
212	
213	    async def _loopback_call(
214	        self,
215	        prompt: str,
216	        system: str,
217	        call_type: str,
218	        schema_name: Optional[str],
219	        schema_json: Optional[dict],
220	        max_tokens: int,
221	        temperature: float,
222	        timeout: Optional[float],
223	        reasoning_exclude: Optional[bool],
224	    ) -> LLMResponse:
225	        req_id = uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]
226	        timeout_sec = timeout or DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SEC
227	        req_path = PENDING_DIR / f"req_{req_id}.json"
228	        resp_path = RESPONSES_DIR / f"resp_{req_id}.json"
229	        start_ts = time.time()
230	
231	        request = {
232	            "request_id": req_id,
233	            "call_type": call_type,
234	            "schema_name": schema_name,
235	            "schema_json": schema_json,
236	            "system": system,
237	            "prompt": prompt,
238	            "max_tokens": max_tokens,
239	            "temperature": temperature,
240	            "reasoning_exclude": reasoning_exclude,
241	            "timestamp": start_ts,
242	        }
243	        with open(req_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
244	            json.dump(request, f, indent=2)
245	
246	        # Banner so the operator sees this in the launching shell
247	        banner = (
248	            f"[LOOPBACK] PENDING req_{req_id} call_type={call_type} "
249	            f"prompt={len(prompt)}c system={len(system)}c max_tokens={max_tokens}"
250	        )
251	        logger.info(banner)
252	        print(banner, flush=True)
253	
254	        deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout_sec
255	        while time.monotonic() < deadline:
256	            if resp_path.exists():
257	                try:
258	                    with open(resp_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
259	                        response = json.load(f)
260	                except (json.JSONDecodeError, PermissionError, OSError) as exc:
261	                    # File still being written, or Windows file-lock race
262	                    # (errno 13 when the writer process still holds the
263	                    # handle). Keep polling — the writer will release it.
264	                    logger.debug(
265	                        "[LOOPBACK] resp_%s read retry: %s",
266	                        req_id, type(exc).__name__,
267	                    )
268	                    await asyncio.sleep(POLL_INTERVAL_SEC)
269	                    continue
270	
271	                # Move both files to done/ for audit trail. On Windows the
272	                # rename can also race with antivirus handles — retry a few
273	                # times before giving up (archival is best-effort).
274	                for rename_attempt in range(5):
275	                    try:
276	                        if req_path.exists():
277	                            req_path.rename(DONE_DIR / req_path.name)
278	                        if resp_path.exists():
279	                            resp_path.rename(DONE_DIR / resp_path.name)
280	                        break
281	                    except (PermissionError, OSError) as exc:
282	                        if rename_attempt == 4:
283	                            logger.warning(
284	                                "[LOOPBACK] Could not archive %s after retries: %s",
285	                                req_id, exc,
286	                            )
287	                        else:
288	                            await asyncio.sleep(0.2)
289	
290	                content = response.get("content", "")
291	                reasoning = response.get("reasoning", "") or ""
292	                input_tokens = response.get("input_tokens", max(1, len(prompt) // 4))
293	                output_tokens = response.get("output_tokens", max(1, len(content) // 4))
294	                duration_ms = (time.time() - start_ts) * 1000
295	
296	                # Record in usage tracker
297	                self.usage.record(
298	                    call_type=call_type,
299	                    input_tokens=input_tokens,
300	                    output_tokens=output_tokens,
301	                    duration_ms=duration_ms,
302	                    api_cost=0.0,  # operator is free
303	                    # FX-11 (I-ready-017 P2b): free=True ledgers cost 0 instead of a phantom
304	                    # paid-rate token estimate, and (with no _add_run_cost) keeps ledger==run-total.
305	                    free=True,
306	                )
307	
308	                logger.info(
309	                    "[LOOPBACK] RESOLVED req_%s in %.1fs (content=%dc, reasoning=%dc)",
310	                    req_id, duration_ms / 1000, len(content), len(reasoning),
311	                )
312	
313	                return LLMResponse(
314	                    content=content,
315	                    reasoning=reasoning,
316	                    input_tokens=input_tokens,
317	                    output_tokens=output_tokens,
318	                    model=self.model,
319	                    duration_ms=duration_ms,
320	                    raw_response={"loopback": True, "request_id": req_id},
321	                )
322	
323	            await asyncio.sleep(POLL_INTERVAL_SEC)
324	
325	        raise TimeoutError(
326	            f"[LOOPBACK] No resp_{req_id}.json after {timeout_sec:.0f}s "
327	            f"(call_type={call_type})"
328	        )
329

1	"""I-arch-005 B14 (#1257) — shared SIDE-JUDGE empty-content guard + retry + IO trace.
2	
3	A *side judge* is any non-binding evaluator LLM call that is NOT one of the four locked
4	faithfulness roles (e.g. the cross-document NLI conflict judge, the credibility judge,
5	the entailment side-judge). Their shared failure mode (forensically observed mid-run on
6	drb_72 / drb_75, 2026-06-12..14) is the **empty-content collapse**: a reasoning model
7	(GLM-5.1) burns its whole token budget on hidden reasoning and returns a 200 with an
8	EMPTY / None / whitespace ``message.content``. Downstream that becomes ``json.loads(None)``
9	(a ``TypeError``) → a broad ``except`` → either a silent fail-open ('neutral', 0.0) that
10	DROPS a possible real finding, or a fail-CLOSED raise that HOLDS the whole report.
11	
12	This module is the ONE place that:
13	  (a) detects empty/None/whitespace content (``is_empty_content``);
14	  (b) RETRIES the call on empty content (inheriting the proven 4-role transport retry
15	      posture — see ``semantic_conflict_detector`` empty-content note + the existing
16	      retries; here the retry is a fresh re-invocation of the injected call);
17	  (c) captures the raw request/response of EVERY attempt to a trace (and, when a
18	      run-scoped raw-IO sink is active, to ``<run_dir>/llm_io/`` — the empty-content
19	      upstream cause is currently INFERENCE; this capture exists to CONFIRM it);
20	  (d) on persistent empty returns a typed ``JudgeUnavailable`` sentinel — it NEVER
21	      raises. "VERIFY = LABEL, NEVER HOLD" (operator-locked 2026-06-14): a side-judge
22	      infra failure may only ADD a disclosed label, never prevent artifact emission.
23	
24	POSTURE (binding):
25	  * PURE-ADDITIVE. No production caller is changed by importing this module; callers opt
26	    in. It NEVER widens a span, never flips ``is_verified``, never makes a failing claim
27	    pass — it only converts an empty-content judge failure into a disclosed *label*.
28	  * The LLM call is DEPENDENCY-INJECTED (``call_fn``) so the guard is fully offline-
29	    testable with a deterministic fake (no network, no model, no spend).
30	  * NEVER raises for an empty/None result. It DOES re-raise a caller-designated
31	    "propagate" exception class (e.g. ``BudgetExceededError``) unchanged — a budget cap
32	    breach is a clean stop signal, not an unadjudicated side-judge call.
33	  * LAW VI: the retry count is an env-overridable named constant (no magic number).
34	"""
35	from __future__ import annotations
36	
37	import logging
38	import os
39	import uuid
40	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
41	from typing import Any, Callable
42	
43	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
44	
45	# LAW VI — env-overridable retry budget. Default 2 retries (3 total attempts): the
46	# empty-content collapse is intermittent (a single provider hiccup), so a small bounded
47	# retry recovers the common case without inflating spend. 0 => one attempt, no retry.
48	_ENV_RETRIES = "PG_SIDE_JUDGE_EMPTY_RETRIES"
49	_DEFAULT_RETRIES = 2
50	
51	
52	def side_judge_empty_retries() -> int:
53	    """Resolve the empty-content retry budget (LAW VI). Non-int / negative => default."""
54	    try:
55	        n = int(os.environ.get(_ENV_RETRIES, "") or _DEFAULT_RETRIES)
56	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
57	        return _DEFAULT_RETRIES
58	    return n if n >= 0 else _DEFAULT_RETRIES
59	
60	
61	def is_empty_content(content: Any) -> bool:
62	    """True iff ``content`` is None / not a string / empty / whitespace-only.
63	
64	    This is the exact predicate the downstream ``json.loads(content)`` chokes on:
65	    ``json.loads(None)`` raises ``TypeError`` and ``json.loads("")`` /
66	    ``json.loads("   ")`` raise ``json.JSONDecodeError`` — every one is an
67	    empty-content collapse, not a real verdict. Kept total (never raises) so it is
68	    safe to call on any raw provider payload.
69	    """
70	    if content is None:
71	        return True
72	    if not isinstance(content, str):
73	        return True
74	    return content.strip() == ""
75	
76	
77	@dataclass
78	class JudgeUnavailable:
79	    """Typed sentinel: the side judge could not produce non-empty content after retries.
80	
81	    This is a *value*, not an exception — the whole point (operator-locked 2026-06-14):
82	    a side-judge infra failure must be LABELABLE, never raisable into a report HOLD. The
83	    caller converts this into a disclosed gap label (e.g. ``conflict_unscored`` /
84	    ``credibility_unscored``); it asserts NOTHING about the underlying claim — only that
85	    the judge could not adjudicate.
86	
87	    ``attempts`` is the number of calls made; ``reason`` is a short human string;
88	    ``last_response`` is the final raw provider payload (for the forensic trace).
89	    """
90	
91	    reason: str
92	    attempts: int = 0
93	    last_response: Any = None
94	
95	
96	@dataclass
97	class SideJudgeAttempt:
98	    """One captured request/response attempt (the in-memory forensic trace row)."""
99	
100	    call_id: str
101	    attempt: int
102	    request: Any
103	    raw_response: Any
104	    empty: bool
105	
106	
107	@dataclass
108	class SideJudgeOutcome:
109	    """The guard result: either a parsed ``value`` or a ``JudgeUnavailable`` sentinel.
110	
111	    ``value`` is the injected ``call_fn``'s return on the FIRST non-empty attempt (the
112	    caller parses it). ``unavailable`` is set iff every attempt returned empty content.
113	    ``trace`` is the list of every attempt's captured request/response (always present
114	    so a test can assert raw IO was captured even on the success path).
115	    """
116	
117	    value: Any = None
118	    unavailable: JudgeUnavailable | None = None
119	    trace: list = field(default_factory=list)
120	
121	    @property
122	    def is_unavailable(self) -> bool:
123	        return self.unavailable is not None
124	
125	
126	def call_side_judge_with_guard(
127	    call_fn: Callable[[], Any],
128	    *,
129	    extract_content: Callable[[Any], Any],
130	    call_type: str,
131	    role: str | None = None,
132	    build_request: Callable[[], Any] | None = None,
133	    propagate: tuple = (),
134	    retries: int | None = None,
135	    io_sink: Any = None,
136	) -> SideJudgeOutcome:
137	    """Call a side judge with empty-content retry + raw-IO capture + a no-raise sentinel.
138	
139	    Parameters
140	    ----------
141	    call_fn:
142	        Zero-arg callable that performs ONE side-judge LLM call and returns its RAW
143	        provider response (the thing ``extract_content`` reads). Injected so the guard is
144	        offline-testable. May raise a ``propagate`` exception (re-raised unchanged) — any
145	        OTHER exception is captured as an empty/failed attempt (NEVER re-raised).
146	    extract_content:
147	        ``raw_response -> content`` projector (e.g.
148	        ``lambda r: r["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]``). Run under a guard so a
149	        malformed shape counts as empty, not a crash.
150	    call_type / role:
151	        Forensic tags for the raw-IO sink record.
152	    build_request:
153	        Optional zero-arg callable returning the request body to record (for the trace).
154	        Kept lazy so the caller can capture the exact final body per attempt.
155	    propagate:
156	        Exception classes that MUST escape unchanged (e.g. ``BudgetExceededError``).
157	    retries:
158	        Override the env retry budget (tests). ``None`` => ``side_judge_empty_retries()``.
159	    io_sink:
160	        Optional run-scoped raw-IO sink (``LlmIoSink``). ``None`` => only the active
161	        ambient sink (``openrouter_client.current_raw_io_sink()``) is used, if any.
162	
163	    Returns
164	    -------
165	    SideJudgeOutcome with EITHER ``value`` (first non-empty response) OR ``unavailable``
166	    (a ``JudgeUnavailable`` after all attempts returned empty). Never raises except for a
167	    ``propagate`` class.
168	    """
169	    n_retries = side_judge_empty_retries() if retries is None else max(0, int(retries))
170	    total_attempts = n_retries + 1
171	    trace: list = []
172	    last_response: Any = None
173	
174	    sink = io_sink
175	    if sink is None:
176	        try:
177	            from src.polaris_graph.llm import openrouter_client as _orc  # noqa: PLC0415
178	            sink = _orc.current_raw_io_sink()
179	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — sink discovery must never break the call
180	            sink = None
181	
182	    for attempt in range(1, total_attempts + 1):
183	        request_body: Any = None
184	        if build_request is not None:
185	            try:
186	                request_body = build_request()
187	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — request capture must never break the call
188	                request_body = None
189	        call_id = uuid.uuid4().hex
190	        empty = True
191	        raw_response: Any = None
192	        try:
193	            raw_response = call_fn()
194	        except propagate:  # type: ignore[misc]  # a designated clean-stop signal — re-raise
195	            raise
196	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a transport/parse failure is an empty attempt
197	            logger.warning(
198	                "[side-judge:%s] attempt %d/%d raised (treated as empty): %s",
199	                call_type, attempt, total_attempts, exc,
200	            )
201	            raw_response = {"error": str(exc)}
202	            empty = True
203	        else:
204	            try:
205	                content = extract_content(raw_response)
206	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — malformed shape == empty content
207	                content = None
208	            empty = is_empty_content(content)
209	        last_response = raw_response
210	
211	        # Capture EVERY attempt's request/response — empty or not — to the forensic trace
212	        # (and the run-scoped raw-IO sink when active). The empty-content upstream cause is
213	        # currently INFERENCE; this capture is how we CONFIRM it. Best-effort, never raises.
214	        trace.append(SideJudgeAttempt(
215	            call_id=call_id, attempt=attempt, request=request_body,
216	            raw_response=raw_response, empty=empty,
217	        ))
218	        if sink is not None:
219	            try:
220	                sink.record(
221	                    call_id=call_id,
222	                    call_type=call_type,
223	                    role=role,
224	                    request=request_body,
225	                    raw_response=raw_response,
226	                    duration_ms=None,
227	                    status=("ok" if not empty else "empty_content"),
228	                )
229	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — capture must never perturb the call
230	                pass
231	
232	        if not empty:
233	            return SideJudgeOutcome(value=raw_response, unavailable=None, trace=trace)
234	
235	    # Every attempt returned empty content → a typed sentinel, NEVER a raise.
236	    logger.warning(
237	        "[side-judge:%s] persistent empty content after %d attempt(s) -> JudgeUnavailable "
238	        "(LABEL, never HOLD)", call_type, total_attempts,
239	    )
240	    return SideJudgeOutcome(
241	        value=None,
242	        unavailable=JudgeUnavailable(
243	            reason=f"{call_type}: empty content after {total_attempts} attempt(s)",
244	            attempts=total_attempts,
245	            last_response=last_response,
246	        ),
247	        trace=trace,
248	    )
249

1	"""Token-limit resolver (B10 governance — fixes the judge HTTP-400 token-starvation).
2	
3	PROBLEM (operator-locked 2026-06-14, B10 lane):
4	    A role/judge call sets ``max_tokens`` to a large generous budget (correct per
5	    §9.1.8 "go max"), but a model's context window is ``prompt_tokens + max_tokens``.
6	    When ``prompt_tokens + max_tokens`` exceeds the serving provider's context
7	    window (or its per-request completion cap), OpenRouter returns HTTP-400
8	    ("max_tokens ... exceeds context length"). That 400 is exactly what HELD the
9	    A1/A2 qwen-judge report — a generous cap that should have been free insurance
10	    instead aborted the run.
11	
12	THE FIX (a CEILING, never a target):
13	    ``allowed_max_tokens = min(provider_completion_cap,
14	                               context_length - prompt_tokens - safety_margin)``
15	    so ``max_tokens`` NEVER consumes the whole context window. This is a
16	    clamp-DOWN-only ceiling applied as the FINAL mutation of ``body["max_tokens"]``
17	    in ``openrouter_client._call_impl`` (after the §9.1.8 reasoning-first floor/cap
18	    chain). Reasoning EFFORT stays MAX (§9.1.8) — this only bounds the *completion
19	    token allotment* so the request is well-formed.
20	
21	DESIGN INVARIANTS (so the off/offline/generator paths stay byte-identical):
22	    1. PASS-THROUGH when metadata is unavailable. If we cannot resolve a model's
23	       limits (offline, model absent from the /models table, fetch disabled), the
24	       requested ``max_tokens`` is returned UNCHANGED. The generator's 384000
25	       budget on a validated full-cap provider therefore never clamps.
26	    2. CLAMP-DOWN ONLY. ``resolved <= requested`` always; we never RAISE a budget
27	       (raising could re-introduce a different provider's 404/400).
28	    3. FAIL-LOUD, never silent-starve (LAW II / §9.1.8). When the prompt genuinely
29	       does not fit (the prompt alone, plus the safety margin, overruns the context
30	       window so no positive completion budget is well-formed), we RAISE
31	       ``PromptTooLargeError`` and LOUD-log — a tiny max_tokens could not make an
32	       over-window prompt well-formed (the request would still 400), so we surface
33	       the real cause deterministically rather than fire a doomed request or
34	       silently starve. We do NOT re-apply the reasoning-first floor after a clamp
35	       (that would re-create the 400 this module exists to prevent).
36	    4. OFFLINE-SAFE + CACHED. The /api/v1/models table is fetched once, lazily,
37	       behind a TTL cache, via a separately-monkeypatchable fetch hook so tests
38	       INJECT metadata and never touch the network. prompt_tokens is a cheap
39	       char/4 estimate (no heavy tokenizer — §8.4).
40	    5. ENV-GOVERNED (LAW VI). A kill-switch (``PG_TOKEN_LIMIT_RESOLVER``, default
41	       ON) and the safety margin (``PG_TOKEN_LIMIT_SAFETY_MARGIN``) are env knobs.
42	
43	This module is import-safe: it performs NO network I/O at import time.
44	"""
45	
46	from __future__ import annotations
47	
48	import json
49	import logging
50	import os
51	import time
52	import urllib.request
53	from typing import Optional
54	
55	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris.llm.token_limit_resolver")
56	
57	
58	class PromptTooLargeError(ValueError):
59	    """The prompt alone (plus the safety margin) overruns the model's context window.
60	
61	    Raised by compute_allowed_max_tokens when no positive completion budget can
62	    make the request well-formed. This is a deterministic, LOUD prompt-too-large
63	    failure (LAW II / §9.1.8 never-starve) — clamping max_tokens to a tiny value
64	    would NOT help (the prompt still exceeds context → the request still 400s), so
65	    we surface the real cause instead of firing a doomed request or silently
66	    starving. The caller decides how to degrade (e.g. an honest aborted section).
67	    """
68	
69	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
70	# Env-governed knobs (LAW VI)
71	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
72	
73	# Master kill-switch. Default ON. When "0", compute_allowed_max_tokens is a
74	# byte-identical pass-through (returns the requested value unchanged) — the
75	# rollback / regression switch the diff-gate can verify.
76	_ENABLED_ENV = "PG_TOKEN_LIMIT_RESOLVER"
77	
78	# Safety margin (tokens) reserved below the context window so prompt + completion
79	# + provider framing overhead never tips into a 400. Generous default; env-tunable.
80	_SAFETY_MARGIN_ENV = "PG_TOKEN_LIMIT_SAFETY_MARGIN"
81	_DEFAULT_SAFETY_MARGIN = 2048
82	
83	# /api/v1/models table cache TTL (seconds). The table is large and changes rarely.
84	_MODELS_TTL_ENV = "PG_TOKEN_LIMIT_MODELS_TTL_SECONDS"
85	_DEFAULT_MODELS_TTL = 3600
86	
87	# Whether the resolver may fetch the live /api/v1/models table at all. Default ON;
88	# set "0" in fully-offline runs to rely solely on the static fallback table.
89	_ALLOW_FETCH_ENV = "PG_TOKEN_LIMIT_ALLOW_FETCH"
90	
91	_MODELS_URL = os.getenv(
92	    "OPENROUTER_BASE_URL", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
93	).rstrip("/") + "/models"
94	
95	
96	def _resolver_enabled() -> bool:
97	    return os.getenv(_ENABLED_ENV, "1") == "1"
98	
99	
100	def _safety_margin() -> int:
101	    try:
102	        return max(0, int(os.getenv(_SAFETY_MARGIN_ENV, str(_DEFAULT_SAFETY_MARGIN))))
103	    except ValueError:
104	        return _DEFAULT_SAFETY_MARGIN
105	
106	
107	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
108	# Static fallback limits (offline-safe). Conservative, verified figures for the
109	# locked-stack roles so the resolver still bounds a request when the live table
110	# is unreachable. A model ABSENT from both the live table AND this map resolves
111	# to None -> PASS-THROUGH (the requested value is honored unchanged).
112	#
113	# These are APPROXIMATE offline-fallback figures (NOT read from /api/v1/models in
114	# this session — do not treat them as authoritative; the LIVE table is the source
115	# of truth per CLAUDE.md §9.1.8 "read the API, don't guess"). They are chosen
116	# LOWER-bound-safe: a real provider with a bigger window only means we under-clamp
117	# slightly (still well-formed), never over-clamp. When the live table is reachable
118	# it overrides these entirely.
119	#
120	# CAVEAT (advisor B10): the per-model completion cap stored here / read from the
121	# live table is OpenRouter's ``top_provider.max_completion_tokens`` — a
122	# popularity-ranked field, NOT necessarily the GENUINELY-SERVED provider's cap.
123	# The spec's "overlay the serving provider cap" is therefore only partially met:
124	# the caller compensates for the one case where it matters (the provider-pinned
125	# reasoning-first generator family) via ``apply_completion_cap=False``. Resolving
126	# the true served-provider cap per /models/<id>/endpoints is a residual refinement.
127	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
128	
129	# model_slug -> (context_length, provider_completion_cap_or_None)
130	#
131	# Codex B10 iter-1 P1-2: the GENERATOR is DELIBERATELY ABSENT from this static
132	# table. The generator runs on the validated full-cap fp8 chain at the §9.1.8
133	# 384000 budget; a conservative static context here would clamp that 384000 DOWN
134	# offline (a #1253 regression). Absent -> resolve_model_limits returns None ->
135	# PASS-THROUGH (byte-identical), which is exactly right for the generator. The
136	# static fallback covers ONLY the verifier/judge roles whose smaller windows are
137	# the genuine binding constraint (the qwen-judge 400 this module fixes). When the
138	# live /models table IS reachable it supplies the generator's true (large) limits,
139	# which also never clamp the 384000 request.
140	_STATIC_MODEL_LIMITS: dict[str, tuple[int, Optional[int]]] = {
141	    # Mirror / evaluator / side-judges.
142	    "z-ai/glm-5.1":               (200000, 131072),
143	    # Sentinel.
144	    "minimax/minimax-m2":         (204800, 131072),
145	    # Judge — the qwen-400 victim. A2/A3 RC2 (iarch007): the prior offline fallback was the
146	    # MIS-STATED (131072 ctx, 65536 cap), which over-clamped the judge to 65536 offline and did
147	    # NOT match the REAL serving window. The role-transport judge provider chain (wandb 262144,
148	    # io-net 262140) serves a 262144 context window with a ~262144 completion cap, so the offline
149	    # fallback is the REAL window (262144 ctx, 262144 cap). The clamp then reconciles a generous
150	    # max_tokens DOWN against the true window (ctx - prompt - margin) — the actual qwen-judge
151	    # HTTP-400 fix — instead of mis-clamping to a stale 65536. The LIVE /models table still
152	    # overrides this when reachable. (Retained: the canonical-pinned lock's sovereign Judge is
153	    # still qwen, pending an operator-signed reconciliation — see I-judge-kimi.)
154	    "qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b":       (262144, 262144),
155	    # Judge (ACTIVE benchmark, I-judge-kimi 2026-06-29): moonshotai/kimi-k2.6. Live /models +
156	    # /endpoints 2026-06-29: context_length 262144, top_provider.max_completion_tokens 262144. The
157	    # offline fallback mirrors the live values so an offline clamp reconciles a generous max_tokens
158	    # DOWN against the true 262144 window (ctx - prompt - margin), never a stale figure. The LIVE
159	    # /models table overrides this when reachable.
160	    "moonshotai/kimi-k2.6":       (262144, 262144),
161	}
162	
163	
164	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
165	# Live /api/v1/models table — lazily fetched, TTL-cached, monkeypatchable.
166	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
167	
168	# Cache: model_slug -> (context_length, provider_completion_cap_or_None)
169	_models_cache: Optional[dict[str, tuple[int, Optional[int]]]] = None
170	_models_cache_at: float = 0.0
171	
172	
173	def _fetch_models_table() -> Optional[list[dict]]:
174	    """Fetch the raw OpenRouter /api/v1/models ``data`` list.
175	
176	    Returns the list of model dicts, or None on any failure (offline, non-200,
177	    malformed). Tests monkeypatch THIS function to inject metadata without
178	    touching the network. NEVER raises — a fetch failure must degrade to
179	    pass-through, not abort the LLM call.
180	    """
181	    if os.getenv(_ALLOW_FETCH_ENV, "1") != "1":
182	        return None
183	    try:
184	        req = urllib.request.Request(
185	            _MODELS_URL,
186	            headers={"User-Agent": "polaris-token-limit-resolver"},
187	        )
188	        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as resp:  # nosec B310 (https slug)
189	            if getattr(resp, "status", 200) != 200:
190	                return None
191	            payload = json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8"))
192	        data = payload.get("data")
193	        if isinstance(data, list):
194	            return data
195	        return None
196	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fetch must never abort the call
197	        logger.warning(
198	            "[token-limit-resolver] /models fetch failed (%s); "
199	            "falling back to static limits / pass-through",
200	            type(exc).__name__,
201	        )
202	        return None
203	
204	
205	def _parse_models_table(data: list[dict]) -> dict[str, tuple[int, Optional[int]]]:
206	    """Reduce the raw /models ``data`` list to {slug: (context_length, completion_cap)}."""
207	    out: dict[str, tuple[int, Optional[int]]] = {}
208	    for entry in data:
209	        try:
210	            slug = entry.get("id")
211	            if not slug:
212	                continue
213	            ctx = entry.get("context_length")
214	            top = entry.get("top_provider") or {}
215	            cap = top.get("max_completion_tokens")
216	            ctx_int = int(ctx) if ctx is not None else None
217	            cap_int = int(cap) if cap is not None else None
218	            if ctx_int is None and cap_int is None:
219	                continue
220	            # If context is unknown but cap is known, treat cap as the bound;
221	            # store a large context so the cap is what binds.
222	            out[slug] = (ctx_int if ctx_int is not None else 10 ** 9, cap_int)
223	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
224	            continue
225	    return out
226	
227	
228	def _get_models_cache() -> dict[str, tuple[int, Optional[int]]]:
229	    """Return the TTL-cached parsed models table (possibly empty)."""
230	    global _models_cache, _models_cache_at
231	    ttl = _DEFAULT_MODELS_TTL
232	    try:
233	        ttl = int(os.getenv(_MODELS_TTL_ENV, str(_DEFAULT_MODELS_TTL)))
234	    except ValueError:
235	        pass
236	    now = time.monotonic()
237	    if _models_cache is not None and (now - _models_cache_at) < ttl:
238	        return _models_cache
239	    raw = _fetch_models_table()
240	    parsed = _parse_models_table(raw) if raw else {}
241	    _models_cache = parsed
242	    _models_cache_at = now
243	    return parsed
244	
245	
246	def reset_cache() -> None:
247	    """Clear the models cache (test hook)."""
248	    global _models_cache, _models_cache_at
249	    _models_cache = None
250	    _models_cache_at = 0.0
251	
252	
253	def resolve_model_limits(model: str) -> Optional[tuple[int, Optional[int]]]:
